page: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpldsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 ARM64 processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at 3.4 GHz. The system is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage capabilities include 2835 GB of local disk storage alongside Premium Storage compatibility. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate strong multi-core performance and top-tier memory bandwidth. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in large-block memory read (464.03 GB/sec) and write (200.4 GB/sec) bandwidth tests, alongside a top-tier Passmark memory score. Multi-core processing is robust, as evidenced by a strong stress-ng multi-core score of 99380.68 and a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 22029.0. Database and web serving capabilities are also notable, with Passmark database operations scoring in the top tier and static web serving throughput reaching 74.8 GB/sec.

The hardware profile presents specific architectural tradeoffs. While multi-threaded execution and memory throughput are highly efficient, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average tier. The lack of AVX, AVX-512, and AMX instruction sets limits its suitability for vector-heavy mathematical workloads, and large-model LLM inference shows weaker prompt processing speeds. However, the ARM64 architecture offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded, memory-bandwidth-bound workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading.

This server type is highly suited for scale-out multi-threaded applications, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and memory-bound caching layers such as Redis. The combination of 2835 GB of local storage and high database benchmark scores makes it an effective choice for localized data processing, software compilation, and medium-scale containerized microservices.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48plds_v6 is an ARM64-based virtual machine powered by a 48-core Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It features 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage support. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds of 464.03 GB/sec and write speeds of 200.4 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is highly competitive, supported by a Geekbench multi-core score of 22029.0 and strong Redis SET performance. However, single-core execution remains average, and the platform lacks AVX or AMX vector extensions. This server is optimized for multi-threaded workloads, high-throughput database operations, web serving, and caching applications that benefit from high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 48 Neoverse-N2 cores, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v6 is a 48-core ARM64 virtual machine with 96.0 GB RAM and 2835 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 Microsoft Neoverse-N2 physical cores at 3.4 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory and 2835 GB of local storage
Top-tier memory read and write bandwidth for data-intensive tasks
Strong multi-core processing and high-throughput database performance
Optimized for web serving, caching, and multi-threaded applications
